title: ISS Junior Fellowship Programme 2026–2027 in Dakar: Fully Funded Opportunity for Young West African Policy Leaders
summary: Are you a young West African graduate passionate about shaping the future of governance, security, and democracy in your region? The ISS Junior Fellowship Programme 2026–2027 offers a transformative opportunity to gain hands-on experience, influence policy discussions, and build a meaningful career in human security and geopolitics. Organized by the Institute for Security Studies in collaboration with the Bosch Foundation , this prestigious fellowship is designed to empower the next generation of African policy leaders. About the ISS Junior Fellowship Programme The ISS Junior Fellowship Programme is a flagship initiative focused on “Gender, human security and geopolitics.” It aims to equip exceptional young West Africans with the knowledge, skills, and networks needed to address pressing governance and security challenges in the region. Successful applicants will spend 10 months in Dakar , working alongside leading African experts and engaging directly with policymakers and institutions that shape the region’s future. This is not just a fellowship—it is a career-launching platform for individuals committed to driving impactful change in West Africa. Programme Objectives The fellowship is designed to: Strengthen understanding of governance and security challenges in West Africa Promote gender-responsive and inclusive policy solutions Build a new generation of leaders equipped to influence policy and decision-making Foster collaboration between researchers, policymakers, and practitioners What Fellows Will Do Participants in the ISS Junior Fellowship Programme will engage in a wide range of impactful activities, including: Conduct In-Depth Research Explore key security issues such as gender inequality Analyze how regional and global political alliances impact governance Participate in Workshops and Seminars Engage with policymakers and practitioners from across West Africa Gain insights into real-world policy challenges and solutions Publish and Collaborate Write research articles and policy briefs Contribute to collaborative reports with experts and peers Engage in Policy Debates Discuss pressing issues with a multicultural team of analysts Develop critical thinking and policy analysis skills Present Weekly Updates Deliver briefings on political and security developments in the region Build strong communication and presentation skills Interact with Decision Makers Gain direct exposure to government officials and key stakeholders Understand how policies are shaped and implemented Benefits of the Fellowship The ISS Junior Fellowship Programme offers a comprehensive support package to ensure fellows can focus fully on their development: Monthly stipend to cover living expenses Health insurance for the duration of the programme Accommodation in Dakar Return airfare to and from your home country Professional mentorship from experienced ISS researchers Access to a strong professional network across Africa Why You Should Apply This fellowship stands out as a unique opportunity for young West Africans to: Gain practical experience in policy research and analysis Build connections with influential policymakers and institutions Develop expertise in human security, governance, and geopolitics Position themselves for impactful careers in regional and international organizations If you are driven by the desire to make West Africa safer, more democratic, and more accountable, this programme provides the tools and platform to turn your ambitions into reality. Application Requirements To be eligible for the ISS Junior Fellowship Programme, applicants must: Be a citizen of a West African country Be under 30 years of age Hold a Master’s degree in a relevant field Demonstrate strong interest in security, governance, gender, or geopolitics Be fluent in English or French Possess strong communication skills and a proactive attitude Application Process To apply for the fellowship, candidates must submit the following documents via email: A motivational cover letter A detailed CV with three references A copy of their Master’s degree certificate A scanned copy of their ID or passport Applications should be sent to the official ISS recruitment email. Application Deadline The deadline for submitting applications is 10 May 2026 . Late applications will not be considered, so applicants are encouraged to prepare and submit all required documents well in advance. APPLY HERE . Final Thoughts The ISS Junior Fellowship Programme 2026–2027 is a highly competitive and rewarding opportunity for young West Africans who aspire to influence policy and drive change in their region. With its strong focus on research, mentorship, and real-world engagement, this fellowship serves as a powerful stepping stone toward a successful career in policy and governance. For those ready to lead, innovate, and contribute to a safer and more prosperous West Africa, this programme offers the ideal starting point. For more information about this opportunity, visit here . Discover more Global Fellowship Opportunities on OFY here . The post ISS Junior Fellowship Programme 2026–2027 in Dakar: Fully Funded Opportunity for Young West African Policy Leaders appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Youthس Storytelling Challenge 2026 in Egypt: Climate Change Competition for Young Creators, Storytellers, and Youth Leaders
summary: The Youthس Storytelling Challenge 2026 is now officially open for young people across Egypt who want to share their voices, experiences, and creative ideas on climate change and environmental solutions. This exciting youth competition invites participants to explore how climate change affects their daily lives and how local communities are responding with innovative and practical solutions. Through short videos, reels, vlogs, audio stories, digital art, and visual storytelling, Youthس creates a platform where young people can lead important conversations around sustainability, environmental awareness, and climate action. Organized under the Danish-Egyptian Dialogue Initiative (DEDI), and implemented by the United Nations Development Programme Egypt (UNDP Egypt) in collaboration with the Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Denmark, this challenge offers young creators a rare opportunity to showcase their ideas, receive professional storytelling support, and gain visibility for their work. Applications are open until 7 May 2026, making this one of the most exciting youth competitions in Egypt for climate-focused innovation and digital storytelling. What is Youthس? “Youthس” is a creative storytelling challenge designed by youth, for youth. The name combines the English word “Youth” with the Arabic letter “س”, symbolizing being seen, heard, and represented. It reflects the challenge’s cross-cultural spirit and its mission to amplify youth voices across Egypt. Youthس creates a space where personal stories are connected to broader climate realities and where local experiences become part of larger conversations about environmental change and sustainable futures. Rather than simply discussing climate change academically, the challenge encourages participants to show how it appears in everyday life—through agriculture, food systems, waste management, and community action. This initiative is built on the belief that young people are not only affected by climate change, but also hold powerful lived experiences and practical ideas that deserve recognition. Why Youthس Matters The challenge aims to: Amplify youth voices and lived experiences Connect personal stories to wider climate challenges Highlight local solutions and innovative community ideas Create a safe and visible space where youth feel seen, heard, and represented Encourage youth-to-youth dialogue and awareness around environmental issues Strengthen youth leadership in climate action and sustainability Youthس is not just a competition—it is a platform for visibility, empowerment, and youth-led environmental change. Focus Areas Participants must submit ideas connected to one of the following three focus areas: Food and Agriculture Stories may focus on food systems, farming challenges, water scarcity, food security, agricultural sustainability, or how climate change impacts local food production. Waste and Circular Economy Participants can explore waste management challenges, recycling systems, plastic reduction, community waste solutions, sustainable consumption, and circular economy innovations. Climate Change This broader category includes environmental challenges, climate resilience, community adaptation, green innovation, and local responses to climate-related issues. Accepted Creative Formats Youthس encourages creative freedom and welcomes multiple digital storytelling formats, including: Short videos Reels Vlogs Photo stories Digital artwork Audio stories Podcasts Participants are encouraged to choose the format that best represents their story and message. What Applicants Need to Submit At this stage, applicants do not need to submit final completed content. Only an idea and creative vision are required. Applicants must submit: 1. Microsoft Form Application Participants must complete the official Microsoft Form and explain: Their story idea The chosen focus area Preferred creative format The message they want to communicate 2. Short Video Submission Applicants must record a 40–60 second video answering: “Which focus area did you choose, and why did you join Youthس?” The video must be uploaded through the application form and also shared on social media while tagging DEDI and UNDP Egypt. This helps demonstrate motivation, originality, and engagement with the challenge. Selection Criteria Applications will be evaluated based on: Relevance to the challenge themes and focus areas Clarity, originality, and strength of the story idea Ability to communicate the message clearly and engage the audience Understanding of the environmental or climate issue being addressed Inclusion of practical solutions, local initiatives, or innovative responses Educational value and audience awareness-building potential Ethical storytelling that respects individuals and communities and avoids harmful or misleading portrayls Strong applications combine creativity, relevance, and practical impact. What Selected Participants Receive Successful participants will gain access to: Professional storytelling capacity-building support Training and mentorship sessions Time and guidance to produce final content Opportunities to showcase their work publicly Possibility to participate in future DEDI and UNDP activities Official certificates of participation upon successful completion This makes Youthس both a competition and a youth development opportunity. Timeline Challenge Launch: 15 April 2026 Application Deadline: 7 May 2026 Selected Participants Announced: 19 May 2026 Capacity Building: May – June 2026 Content Production: June 2026 Final Announcements: July 2026 Early application is strongly recommended. About DEDI The Danish-Egyptian Dialogue Initiative (DEDI) 2025–2028 is a youth-driven platform for innovation, green transition, climate dialogue, peace, and sustainability. Implemented by UNDP Egypt in collaboration with the Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Denmark, DEDI serves as a living laboratory for climate action and cross-cultural dialogue between Denmark and Egypt. It focuses on empowering youth to lead conversations and solutions around the future of sustainability, peace, and environmental justice. How to Apply Interested applicants must complete the official Microsoft Form registration before 7 May 2026. Applicants should prepare: Their story idea and concept Chosen focus area Preferred creative format A 40–60 second personal video response Social media post tagging DEDI and UNDP Egypt Applications must be submitted before the official deadline. For updates and announcements, applicants should follow the official DEDI, UNDP Egypt, and social media platforms. For more information click here APPLY HERE Discover more opportunities here The post Youthس Storytelling Challenge 2026 in Egypt: Climate Change Competition for Young Creators, Storytellers, and Youth Leaders appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Traineeship Call 2026 at the European Union Agency for Fundamental Rights (FRA): Launch Your EU Career in Vienna
summary: Are you a recent graduate eager to gain meaningful international experience while contributing to human rights across Europe? The European Union Agency for Fundamental Rights (FRA) has officially opened applications for its Traineeship Call 2026 , offering a unique opportunity to work at the heart of EU policymaking in Vienna, Austria. This traineeship is more than just work experience—it’s a gateway to understanding how fundamental rights are protected, promoted, and implemented across the European Union. About the European Union Agency for Fundamental Rights (FRA) The European Union Agency for Fundamental Rights (FRA) plays a crucial role in ensuring that fundamental rights are upheld across EU member states. The agency: Provides independent, evidence-based advice to policymakers Collects and analyzes data on fundamental rights issues Supports EU institutions and national governments Raises awareness and promotes rights-based approaches Strengthens cooperation among key human rights stakeholders Working at FRA means contributing to real-world policy impact while learning from leading experts in the field. Overview of the FRA Traineeship 2026 The FRA traineeship is designed for recent graduates looking to gain first-hand experience within an EU agency. Key Details: Location: Vienna, Austria (with one position in Brussels, Belgium) Duration: 10 months (1 October 2026 – 31 July 2027) Contract Type: Traineeship Reference: FRA-TRAI-2026 Monthly Salary: €1,641.22 Working Mode: Full-time, on-site Available Traineeship Areas FRA offers placements across multiple departments, allowing applicants to align their interests with specialized fields: 1. Director’s Office (DIR) Director’s Office Sector Planning Sector Fundamental Rights Forum 2027 2. Communications and Awareness Raising (CAR) Media and Public Relations Web and Publishing 3. Equality, Inclusion and Sustainability (EIS) Equality and Non-Discrimination Climate and Sustainability Age and Social Inclusion 4. Institutional Cooperation and EU Charter (INST) Rule of Law and Democracy National Partnerships and Networks Institutional Relations and Policy Donor-funded Programmes and Enlargement Brussels Liaison Office 5. Justice, Digital and Migration (JDM) Justice and Security Data and Digital Migration and Asylum 6. Administration (ADMIN) Digital Services and Facilities Finance and Contracting Human Resources and Legal 7. Data Protection Officer (DPO) Each unit offers diverse responsibilities, giving trainees exposure to policy development, research, communication, and operational support. What You Will Gain Participating in the FRA traineeship provides numerous professional and personal benefits: Hands-on experience in EU policymaking and human rights work Exposure to international and multicultural work environments Opportunity to collaborate with experts and policymakers Insight into career paths within EU institutions Development of research, communication, and analytical skills Eligibility Criteria To qualify for the FRA Traineeship 2026, applicants must meet the following requirements: Nationality Requirement: EU Member States (27 countries) EEA countries (Iceland, Liechtenstein, Norway) EU candidate countries (e.g., Albania, Serbia, Turkey, Ukraine, etc.) Potential candidate countries (e.g., Kosovo) Education: Must have completed university studies Degree obtained between 30 September 2023 and 1 September 2026 Language: Strong command of English ( minimum B2 level ) Experience: No prior work experience required Special Consideration: Candidates of Roma background may qualify with alternative criteria (secondary education + 2 years of experience) Application Process Read more and apply here Deadline The deadline for applications is: 04 May 2026 at 13:00 (CET) Late submissions will not be accepted, so applicants are strongly encouraged to apply early. Equal Opportunities and Inclusion FRA is committed to diversity and inclusion. The agency: Encourages applications from all backgrounds Ensures non-discriminatory recruitment processes Supports candidates with disabilities through reasonable accommodations Promotes equal participation regardless of gender, ethnicity, or beliefs Data Protection All applicant data is handled in compliance with EU Regulation 2018/1725 , ensuring: Confidentiality Secure data processing Use strictly for recruitment purposes Why You Should Apply If you re passionate about human rights, policy development, and international collaboration, this traineeship offers an unmatched opportunity to: Build a strong foundation for a career in EU institutions Contribute to meaningful societal impact Gain global exposure and professional growth Click here to explore more global Internship opportunities on OFY The post Traineeship Call 2026 at the European Union Agency for Fundamental Rights (FRA): Launch Your EU Career in Vienna appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Grand Challenges Grant Opportunities 2026: Global Funding for Health Innovation, Nutrition Research, AI Solutions, and Development Breakthroughs
summary: The Grand Challenges family of initiatives has officially opened multiple international grant opportunities for 2026 aimed at supporting breakthrough innovations in global health, nutrition, maternal health, diagnostics, artificial intelligence, and development solutions. These highly competitive grants are designed to fund bold ideas that can significantly improve health outcomes, reduce treatment costs, strengthen healthcare systems, and create scalable solutions for vulnerable populations worldwide. Grand Challenges is globally recognized for supporting transformative innovations that tackle some of the world’s most urgent development priorities. Researchers, innovators, institutions, startups, and non-profit organizations are encouraged to apply with solutions that can create measurable impact in low-resource settings and underserved communities. For 2026, several new funding calls focus on severe acute malnutrition treatment, diagnostic screening tools, AI for charitable giving, fetal growth improvement, ready-to-use therapeutic food innovation, and micronutrient absorption from fortified foods. Applications are currently open from 17 March 2026 to 28 April 2026, making this a major opportunity for global innovators working in health and development. Open Grant Opportunities Available in 2026 The following Grand Challenges initiatives are currently accepting applications: Health and Nutrition Innovation Grants Funding is available for breakthrough innovations that significantly reduce the cost of severe acute malnutrition treatment and improve access to life-saving therapeutic interventions. These grants focus on creating affordable, scalable, and sustainable solutions for children and vulnerable populations suffering from malnutrition, especially in low-income and fragile settings. Projects that improve treatment delivery, reduce production costs, and strengthen nutritional recovery pathways are highly encouraged. Application Deadline: 28 April 2026 Diagnostic and Screening Innovation Grants Support is available for cost-disruptive tools for diagnosis and screening that can transform access to early detection and treatment. This includes innovations in medical diagnostics, screening systems, rapid detection tools, and technologies that reduce healthcare costs while improving patient outcomes. Applicants are encouraged to propose accessible and affordable diagnostic solutions that can be deployed in resource-constrained environments. Application Deadline: 28 April 2026 Artificial Intelligence for Social Impact Grants Funding opportunities are open for projects using Artificial Intelligence (AI) to accelerate charitable giving and improve philanthropic systems. This initiative seeks innovative AI-powered solutions that improve donor engagement, strengthen charitable ecosystems, increase giving efficiency, and support social impact organizations. Projects combining ethical AI, development impact, and scalable philanthropic innovation are strongly encouraged. Application Deadline: 28 April 2026 Maternal and Fetal Health Innovation Grants Funding is available for novel interventions targeting placental and gut inflammation to improve fetal growth and maternal health outcomes. These grants support advanced scientific research and translational solutions addressing pregnancy health, inflammation-related complications, and fetal development challenges. Researchers working in maternal health, neonatal care, obstetrics, and biomedical innovation are particularly encouraged to apply. Application Deadline: 28 April 2026 Ready-to-Use Therapeutic Food Innovation Grants Support is available for cost-disrupting innovations that reduce the cost of Ready-to-Use Therapeutic Food (RUTF), which plays a critical role in treating severe acute malnutrition. Applicants should focus on affordability, local production, ingredient innovation, supply chain improvements, and treatment accessibility. This initiative aims to make therapeutic food more accessible for vulnerable populations globally. Application Deadline: 28 April 2026 Micronutrient Absorption and Fortified Foods Grants Funding is available for projects addressing physiological barriers to micronutrient absorption from fortified foods. This initiative focuses on improving nutritional outcomes through better absorption mechanisms, innovative food systems, and interventions that strengthen the effectiveness of fortified foods. Solutions that improve child health, maternal nutrition, and public health outcomes are strongly encouraged. Application Deadline: 28 April 2026 Why You Should Apply These Grand Challenges grants are among the strongest international funding opportunities available in 2026 because they offer: Global recognition for breakthrough innovation Funding for high-impact research and scalable solutions Opportunities to address urgent global health challenges Support for multidisciplinary collaboration Strong international visibility for researchers and institutions Access to global innovation networks Potential for long-term implementation and policy influence Funding opportunities for both early-stage and advanced solutions These grants are ideal for professionals and organizations committed to solving complex health and development challenges with practical, sustainable, and transformative ideas. Whether you are a researcher, startup founder, university team, NGO leader, or innovation specialist, this is a major opportunity to secure global funding and drive meaningful impact. Application Deadline Summary Applications Open: 17 March 2026 Final Deadline: 28 April 2026 Early application is strongly recommended. How to Apply Interested applicants should prepare the following: Detailed project proposal Institutional profile or organizational background Research methodology or innovation framework Budget and implementation strategy Supporting academic or technical documentation Relevant partnerships or collaboration details where applicable Applications must be submitted through the official Grand Challenges application portal. Candidates are strongly advised to carefully review the specific eligibility and thematic focus of each initiative before applying. For more information click here Discover more opportunities here The post Grand Challenges Grant Opportunities 2026: Global Funding for Health Innovation, Nutrition Research, AI Solutions, and Development Breakthroughs appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Commonwealth Secretariat Jobs 2026: Fully Funded International Career Opportunities in London, Fiji, Mauritius, Belize and Across Commonwealth Member States
summary: The Commonwealth Secretariat has officially opened multiple high-level and early-career international job opportunities for 2026, offering exceptional positions for students, young professionals, senior experts, and executive leaders across communications, law, governance, climate finance, economic development, fundraising, and public policy. These opportunities are based in London, Fiji, Mauritius, Belize, Antigua and Barbuda, and across Commonwealth member states in Africa, Asia, the Caribbean, and the Pacific. Whether you are looking for an internship to begin your international career or a senior leadership role in a major intergovernmental organisation, the Commonwealth Secretariat offers one of the strongest global career platforms available in 2026. The Secretariat serves 56 member countries and works to advance democracy, sustainable development, peace, trade, climate resilience, and inclusive economic growth. These vacancies are designed for professionals who want to contribute to global policy and international development while working in one of the world’s most respected diplomatic institutions. Several of these opportunities also offer highly competitive salaries reaching over £120,000 annually, while internships provide paid weekly stipends and valuable professional development. Application deadlines range from 30 April 2026 to 15 May 2026, making this an important recruitment cycle for international candidates. Available Commonwealth Secretariat Job Opportunities 2026 The following vacancies are currently open: Internship Opportunities Communications Intern Location: Marlborough House, Pall Mall, London, UK Salary: £532.80 per week Deadline: 04 May 2026 The Communications Division is seeking a motivated intern to support research, writing, digital communications, stakeholder engagement, analytics reporting, content management systems, and event planning. This role is ideal for candidates interested in international communications, diplomacy, public affairs, and media strategy. Important Requirement: Applicants must be under 30 years old. Digital Communications Intern (6 Months) Location: London, UK Salary: £532.80 per week Deadline: 04 May 2026 This internship supports the Digital Team in producing multimedia content for websites, internal platforms, and social media, especially in preparation for the Commonwealth Heads of Government Meeting (CHOGM). Responsibilities include photography, video production, graphic design, social media writing, CMS management, and campaign support. Important Requirement: Applicants must be under 30 years old. Senior Leadership and Executive Roles Head of Media Relations Salary: £80,672 Deadline: 11 May 2026 This role leads the global media strategy of the Commonwealth Secretariat, overseeing press engagement, crisis communications, hybrid media operations, and strategic storytelling for the Secretary-General and major Commonwealth events. Head of Private Sector Fundraising Salary: £97,512 Deadline: 15 May 2026 This leadership position focuses on expanding funding partnerships with private sector actors, development finance institutions, and international donors to strengthen the organisation’s sustainability. Adviser and Head – Energy and Natural Resources Section Salary: £97,512 Deadline: 15 May 2026 This senior advisory role leads Commonwealth programmes on energy transition, renewable energy, extractive industries governance, and sustainable natural resource development. Frequent international travel is expected. Director of Governance, Strategy and Learning Salary: £120,103 Deadline: 15 May 2026 This high-level position drives the Secretariat’s strategic planning, results-based management, governance systems, and monitoring, evaluation, and learning frameworks. The Director works directly with the Secretary-General and Deputy Secretary-General. General Counsel – Legal Affairs Salary: £120,103 Deadline: 15 May 2026 The General Counsel serves as the Secretariat’s chief legal adviser, leading legal strategy, dispute resolution, litigation, institutional reforms, and international legal compliance. This role requires at least 15 years of post-qualification legal experience. Director Economic Development, Trade and Investment Directorate Salary: £120,103 Deadline: 15 May 2026 This position leads the Commonwealth’s economic development agenda across trade policy, investment governance, debt sustainability, and sustainable development. Candidates with strong experience in international economics and multilateral policy leadership are highly encouraged to apply. Climate Finance Advisory Opportunities Commonwealth Regional Climate Finance Adviser – Indo Pacific Location: Fiji Salary: £57,000 per annum plus allowances and benefits Deadline: 07 May 2026 Commonwealth Regional Climate Finance Adviser – Caribbean Location: Belize Salary: £57,000 per annum plus allowances and benefits Deadline: 07 May 2026 Commonwealth Regional Climate Finance Adviser – Africa Location: Mauritius Salary: £57,000 per annum plus allowances and benefits Deadline: 07 May 2026 Commonwealth Climate Finance Adviser Pool Location: African, Asian, Caribbean and Pacific Member States Salary: £55,000 per annum plus allowances and benefits Deadline: 07 May 2026 Commonwealth Climate Finance Gender Adviser Pool Location: Mauritius Salary: £55,000 per annum plus allowances and benefits Deadline: 07 May 2026 Commonwealth Health Climate Finance Adviser Pool Location: Mauritius Salary: £55,000 per annum plus allowances and benefits Deadline: 07 May 2026 These advisory roles support the Commonwealth Climate Finance Access Hub (CCFAH), which has mobilised over USD 566 million for 180 projects across Commonwealth member states. Successful candidates help countries improve climate resilience, access international climate finance, strengthen institutional readiness, and develop high-quality proposals for sustainable development. Professionals with expertise in climate finance, proposal development, accreditation, gender-responsive financing, and health financing are strongly encouraged to apply. Additional Opportunity Call for Audit and Risk Committee Members Location: London Deadline: 30 April 2026 This voluntary advisory role supports the Commonwealth Secretariat’s Board of Governors in strengthening internal controls, financial integrity, and risk management systems. Applicants must be Commonwealth citizens residing in London and have strong experience in finance, HR, assurance, risk management, or IT governance. The appointment lasts for two years and local travel expenses are reimbursed. Applicants from underrepresented regions, especially Africa and the Pacific, are strongly encouraged to apply. Why You Should Apply These Commonwealth Secretariat jobs are among the strongest international public sector opportunities available in 2026 because they offer: Competitive salaries up to £120,103 annually Paid internships for young professionals International career growth Direct work with governments and global institutions High-level diplomatic exposure Leadership opportunities in global development Climate finance and sustainability impact Access to major Commonwealth policy platforms Career advancement in one of the world’s most respected intergovernmental organisations Whether you are starting your international career or seeking executive leadership, these vacancies provide a rare opportunity to work at the centre of global cooperation. Application Deadline Summary 30 April 2026 — Audit and Risk Committee Members 04 May 2026 — Communications Intern 04 May 2026 — Digital Communications Intern 07 May 2026 — Climate Finance Adviser Roles 11 May 2026 — Head of Media Relations 15 May 2026 — Senior Leadership Roles Early application is strongly recommended. How to Apply Interested applicants should prepare: Updated CV or Resume Strong Cover Letter Relevant professional certifications Writing samples or portfolio where applicable Supporting documents based on the role Applications must be submitted through the official Commonwealth Secretariat recruitment portal. Candidates are advised to carefully review role-specific eligibility before applying. For more information click here Discover more opportunities here The post Commonwealth Secretariat Jobs 2026: Fully Funded International Career Opportunities in London, Fiji, Mauritius, Belize and Across Commonwealth Member States appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Congressional Fellowship Opportunity: Apply Your Scientific Expertise to Shape Public Policy in the U.S.
summary: The Federation of American Scientists is offering an exceptional opportunity for professionals with a strong scientific or technical background to step into the world of policymaking through its prestigious Congressional Fellowship Program . This unique program is designed for individuals eager to bridge the gap between science and legislation, while gaining hands-on experience in the United States legislative process. If you have ever wanted to see how scientific knowledge influences national decision-making—or even be part of that process—this fellowship provides the ideal platform. Overview of the Congressional Fellowship The Congressional Fellowship by the Federation of American Scientists is a full-time, one-year program that places fellows directly within the U.S. Congress. During this time, fellows collaborate with policymakers, contribute to legislative discussions, and provide expert insights on critical science and technology issues. This fellowship is especially valuable for individuals who are passionate about using their academic or professional background to influence real-world policy outcomes. Key Focus Areas As a fellow, you will engage in a variety of pressing and forward-thinking topics at the intersection of science and policy. These include: Artificial Intelligence (AI) Policy Contributing to frameworks that guide ethical and responsible AI use Supporting legislation related to emerging technologies Natural Capital Ecosystem Services Working on environmental sustainability initiatives Advising on policies that value and protect natural resources Government Capacity Innovation Enhancing how government institutions leverage scientific expertise Supporting initiatives that improve efficiency and effectiveness in public service What You Will Do Throughout the fellowship, participants will: Work directly with Congressional offices or committees Conduct research and provide technical expertise on legislative issues Draft policy briefs, reports, and recommendations Engage with stakeholders , including scientists, policymakers, and advocacy groups Develop a deeper understanding of the legislative process in the U.S. Why This Fellowship Matters This program is more than just a professional experience—it’s a chance to make a tangible impact. Here’s what makes it stand out: Real-World Policy Influence Apply your scientific knowledge to shape national and global policies Career Advancement Gain rare experience at the intersection of science, technology, and governance Networking Opportunities Build connections with policymakers, researchers, and industry leaders Skill Development Strengthen your analytical, communication, and policy development skills Competitive Compensation The fellowship offers a competitive salary for the full one-year duration Fellowship Duration Length: 1 Year (Full-Time Commitment) Compensation: Competitive (details provided during application process) How to Apply Interested candidates can learn more about the fellowship and submit their applications through the official link below: https://lnkd.in/gNU3wgZq Final Thoughts The Congressional Fellowship by the Federation of American Scientists is an outstanding opportunity for scientists, engineers, and technical professionals who want to extend their impact beyond research and into policymaking. If you are driven to influence meaningful change and understand how science informs governance, this fellowship could be the perfect next step in your career. title: All-Island Legacy 2026: Fully Funded Leadership Experience in Dublin & Belfast for Young Leaders
summary: The All-Island Legacy 2026 programme is back, offering a transformative and fully sponsored leadership experience for young people across the island of Ireland. Taking place from 5th–8th June 2026 , this unique initiative brings together emerging leaders to explore shared societal challenges, build meaningful connections, and develop essential leadership skills through immersive, real-world experiences. With a strong focus on this year’s theme, “Crossing Boundaries,” the programme aims to inspire collaboration, mutual understanding, and innovative thinking among participants from diverse backgrounds. Overview of All-Island Legacy 2026 All-Island Legacy is a dynamic leadership programme designed to unite young professionals and students from Ireland and Northern Ireland . It builds on the success of previous editions and continues to foster cross-border cooperation and dialogue. Participants will: Engage with influential leaders and changemakers Explore pressing social challenges from an all-island perspective Build sustainable relationships across communities Gain hands-on leadership experience The programme is supported by local sponsorship, making it completely free for selected participants . Programme Dates and Structure The programme runs across three impactful days , combining virtual and in-person experiences: 4th June 2026: Online Kick-Off Session 5th June 2026: Dublin Experience (Hosted at IDA Ireland) 8th June 2026: Belfast Experience (Hosted at Invest NI) Programme Breakdown Day 1: Online Kick-Off Meet fellow participants from across the island Set intentions and expectations Begin exploring shared social challenges Build initial connections and prepare for in-person sessions Day 2: Belfast – Experiencing Change in Action Explore Belfast’s social and civic landscape Meet community leaders and changemakers Understand how collaboration drives social impact Engage in discussions on complex societal issues Learn how influence works beyond formal authority Day 3: Dublin – From Ideas to Action Discover innovative approaches to social challenges Compare perspectives between North and South Collaborate in teams to develop solutions Pitch ideas to a panel of experts Translate insights into actionable strategies Why You Should Apply Participating in All-Island Legacy 2026 offers numerous personal and professional benefits: 1. Build Leadership Skills Develop critical skills such as: Problem-solving Communication Teamwork in diverse environments 2. Expand Your Network Connect with peers across Ireland and Northern Ireland Engage with influential leaders and organizations 3. Gain New Perspectives Understand shared challenges from different viewpoints Explore cross-border collaboration opportunities 4. Strengthen Your Voice Learn to express ideas confidently Improve public speaking and advocacy skills 5. Boost Your Career Profile Add valuable experience to your CV Enhance your LinkedIn profile with leadership credentials 6. Access Practical Tools Gain actionable insights and strategies Apply learning in real-life academic or professional settings Programme Outcomes By the end of the programme, participants will: Demonstrate improved leadership and communication skills Build lasting cross-border relationships Gain a deeper understanding of societal challenges Develop innovative solutions through collaboration Acquire practical tools for continued personal growth Who Can Apply? All-Island Legacy 2026 is open to: Young professionals and students aged 18–25 Individuals living, working, or studying in Ireland or Northern Ireland Cost and Sponsorship Fully funded programme All participation costs are covered through local sponsorship No financial barrier to entry for selected candidates Testimonials from Past Participants Participants from previous editions have described the programme as: “A space that was both challenging and energising… I’m coming away with new perspectives, a wider network, and a stronger sense of what leadership in a connected island can truly look like.” How to Apply Applications for All-Island Legacy 2026 are now open . Apply here Deadline While no specific deadline is explicitly stated, early application is strongly recommended due to high competition and limited slots. Final Thoughts All-Island Legacy 2026 is more than just a leadership programme—it is a platform for young people to shape the future of Ireland through collaboration, innovation, and shared understanding. If you are passionate about leadership, social impact, and cross-border cooperation, this opportunity is designed for you. Learn more about this opportunity: Click here . Explore More Opportunities for Youth Want to discover more Fellowship opportunities? Visit the OFY website: Click here . title: Leiden University Scholarships 2026: Fully Funded and Partial Funding Opportunities for Bachelor’s, Master’s, and Research Students
summary: The Leiden University Student Scholarship Portal provides a wide range of funding opportunities designed to support both current students and prospective applicants. With over 34 scholarships available , these opportunities cater to diverse academic levels, including Bachelor’s, Master’s, PhD, and specialized research programs. This guide explores key scholarships listed on the platform, outlining eligibility, benefits, deadlines, and how students can leverage them to advance their academic and professional goals. Overview of Leiden University Scholarships Leiden University offers scholarships and funding options across multiple faculties and disciplines, including: Archaeology Humanities Medicine (LUMC) Governance and Global Affairs Law Social and Behavioural Sciences Science Interfaculty Institutes These scholarships are designed to: Support international mobility Fund research and academic advancement Provide financial relief for tuition and living costs Enable global academic exposure Featured Scholarships at Leiden University Below are some of the most notable scholarships currently listed: Uhlenbeck Scholarship for Research Master Students Level: Master (Research Master students) Faculty: Humanities Category: Scholarship Description: The Uhlenbeck Scholarship is specifically designed for research master’s students within the Faculty of Humanities. It provides supplementary financial support for students planning to undertake academic research or study abroad as part of their program. Benefits: Financial support for international research stays Enhances global academic exposure Supports specialized research activities Deadline: Ten working days before the start of your stay abroad Wilhelmina E. Jansenfonds Scholarship Level: Master (Graduated MA students) Field: Russian and Eurasian Studies Category: Scholarship Description: This scholarship is offered by the Wilhelmina E. Jansen Foundation and targets graduates of MA programs in Russian and Eurasian Studies. It supports further academic pursuits or research abroad. Benefits: Funding for continued study or research Supports specialization in a niche academic field Encourages international academic collaboration Deadline: To be announced Yenching Academy Fellowship at Peking University Level: Bachelor and Master graduate Category: Scholarship / Fellowship Description: The Yenching Academy Fellowship offers international students the opportunity to pursue a fully funded Master of Arts degree in China Studies at Peking University. This highly competitive program is open to students from all academic backgrounds and faculties. Benefits: Fully funded Master’s degree Access to world-class education in China Studies International networking opportunities Cultural and academic immersion in China Deadline: Monday, 23 November 2026 at 9:00 AM (CET) Who Can Apply? These scholarships are tailored for: Current Leiden University students Prospective international students Bachelor, Master, and PhD candidates Students interested in studying abroad or research opportunities How to Find the Right Scholarship To maximize your chances of success: Identify your eligibility Check your academic level and faculty Filter scholarships accordingly Use the university portal filters (level, faculty, type) Review deadlines carefully Some deadlines are fixed, others vary Prepare required documents early Academic transcripts Motivation letters Research proposals (if applicable) Why Choose Leiden University Scholarships? Access to one of Europe’s leading research universities Opportunities for international exposure and collaboration Wide range of funding options across disciplines Support for academic excellence and innovation Final Thoughts Leiden University continues to provide diverse and impactful scholarship opportunities for students worldwide. Whether you are pursuing undergraduate studies, advanced research, or international academic experiences, these scholarships can significantly reduce financial barriers and open doors to global opportunities. Students are encouraged to regularly check the official scholarship portal and apply well before deadlines to increase their chances of success. title: NASA Crew Radiation Effects Internship 2026 at Johnson Space Center
summary: The NASA Crew Radiation Effects Internship 2026 is a highly specialized research opportunity designed for students who are passionate about space science, human health, and advanced scientific research. Hosted at the Johnson Space Center in Houston, Texas, this internship provides participants with direct exposure to one of the most critical challenges in modern space exploration: understanding the impact of space radiation on human biology. This program is part of NASA’s broader internship initiative aimed at equipping undergraduate and graduate students with practical, hands-on experience in real-world scientific investigations. It is particularly suited for individuals interested in space medicine, radiation biology, aerospace engineering, and related STEM disciplines. Overview of the Internship The internship, officially titled “Literature Review Internship on Human Health Effects of Space Radiation,” focuses on conducting an in-depth review of existing scientific research. Rather than laboratory experimentation, interns will engage in rigorous academic analysis of peer-reviewed studies, technical reports, and scientific publications. Participants will work under the mentorship of experienced researchers and scientists, gaining insight into how scientific knowledge is compiled, evaluated, and used to inform future space missions. Why This Internship Matters Space radiation is one of the biggest barriers to long-duration human spaceflight. Unlike Earth, space environments expose astronauts to high-energy radiation from galactic cosmic rays and solar particle events. These forms of radiation can lead to: Increased cancer risk Cardiovascular complications Damage to the central nervous system Cognitive and neurological impairments As NASA prepares for extended missions to Mars and prolonged stays aboard space stations, understanding and mitigating these risks is essential. This internship directly contributes to that mission by helping synthesize existing knowledge and identify research gaps. Key Responsibilities Interns selected for this program will undertake a wide range of research-focused tasks, including: 1. Scientific Literature Search Use databases such as PubMed, Scopus, and Google Scholar Identify peer-reviewed and high-impact research papers Apply strategic keyword searches related to space radiation and human health 2. Critical Evaluation of Research Analyze study methodologies and experimental designs Evaluate the credibility and relevance of scientific findings Assess objectivity, author expertise, and publication quality 3. Data Organization and Thematic Analysis Categorize research into major themes such as: Biological mechanisms of radiation damage Cancer and cardiovascular risks Neurological and cognitive effects Radiation mitigation strategies 4. Report Writing and Documentation Summarize key findings from selected studies Compile structured literature review reports Contribute to a research database or knowledge repository Learning Outcomes and Benefits This internship is designed to provide both academic and professional development. Participants will gain: Advanced research and analytical skills Hands-on experience in conducting systematic literature reviews Strong scientific writing and communication abilities Familiarity with academic databases and citation tools like Zotero and EndNote A deep understanding of space radiation and its biological effects Additionally, this experience significantly strengthens applications for graduate school, research roles, and future opportunities within the aerospace and scientific communities. Eligibility Criteria Who Can Apply This opportunity is open to: Undergraduate students (Junior and Senior level) Graduate students (Master’s and Doctoral levels) Academic Background Requirements Applicants should be enrolled in a STEM-related field, preferably: Biochemistry, Biophysics, or Molecular Biology Neuroscience or Neurobiology Biomedical or Bioengineering Physics Cognitive or Health Psychology Radiologic or Nuclear Technologies Skills and Competencies Ideal candidates should demonstrate: Strong research and analytical skills Experience with academic literature reviews Familiarity with scientific databases Proficiency in research tools (EndNote, Zotero preferred) Excellent written and verbal communication Ability to work independently and in teams Citizenship Requirement Only United States citizens are eligible to apply This is a strict requirement for NASA internships due to federal regulations. Work Structure and Format Engagement Type: Full-time internship Format: In-person Location: Johnson Space Center, Houston, Texas Duration: Fall 2026 session Interns will be expected to commit fully to the program and actively participate in research activities throughout the internship period. How to Apply Interested candidates must apply through the official NASA internship portal. Applicants should prepare: Academic transcripts Resume or CV Statement of interest Relevant research experience (if available) Apply here Career Impact and Future Opportunities Participation in this internship offers a strong foundation for careers in: Space science and exploration Aerospace medicine Radiation biology Academic and scientific research Government and policy advisory roles The experience also provides networking opportunities with leading scientists and researchers, potentially opening doors to future collaborations or employment within NASA or related organizations. Deadline 22nd May 2026 The NASA Crew Radiation Effects Internship 2026 is an exceptional opportunity for students seeking to contribute to one of the most pressing challenges in human space exploration. By focusing on the biological impact of space radiation, interns play a role in shaping safer and more sustainable space missions. For students passionate about science, research, and the future of humanity beyond Earth, this internship represents a significant step toward a meaningful and impactful career. For more information visit here For more global opportunities click here The post NASA Crew Radiation Effects Internship 2026 at Johnson Space Center appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: EU Digital Europe Programme 2026: €12.5 Million Funding for Advanced Digital Skills Projects Across Europe
summary: The European Commission, in collaboration with European Health and Digital Executive Agency (HaDEA), has officially launched an exciting new funding opportunity under the Digital Europe Programme. This initiative is designed to strengthen advanced digital skills and accelerate innovation across Europe, with a total budget of €12.5 million allocated to support transformative projects. This latest announcement marks the 10th set of open calls under the programme, reinforcing Europe’s commitment to building a digitally skilled workforce and advancing technological capabilities in key sectors such as healthcare and education. Overview of the Funding Opportunity The Digital Europe Programme continues to play a pivotal role in equipping individuals, organizations, and institutions with the digital competencies required to thrive in a rapidly evolving technological landscape. Key Highlights Total Funding Available: €12.5 million Programme: Digital Europe Programme Call Opening Date: 21 April 2026 Application Deadline: 01 October 2026 at 17:00 CEST Focus Area: Advanced Digital Skills Development The funding specifically targets initiatives that promote the deployment and adoption of innovative digital technologies across European Union Member States and associated countries. Detailed Breakdown of the Three Open Calls 1. Advanced Digital Skills for AI Uptake in Health Reference: DIGITAL-2026-SKILLS-10-DIGITAL-HEALTH-STEP This call is dedicated to expanding education and training in Artificial Intelligence (AI) within the healthcare sector . Objectives Enhance AI-related education and training programmes Increase AI readiness among healthcare professionals Support the integration of AI solutions in healthcare environments Who Should Apply Higher education institutions Research organizations Industry stakeholders Expected Impact Improved healthcare delivery through AI innovation Strengthened collaboration between academia and industry Upskilled healthcare workforce ready for digital transformation 2. EdTech Accelerator Reference: DIGITAL-2026-SKILLS-10-EDTECH This call is tailored to support European EdTech startups and SMEs in advancing their digital education solutions. Objectives Transform innovative educational concepts into scalable products Support the development of fully tested and market-ready EdTech solutions Who Should Apply EdTech startups Small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs) Expected Impact Accelerated innovation in digital education Improved access to high-quality digital learning tools Strengthened competitiveness of European EdTech companies 3. National Coalitions for Digital Skills and Jobs Reference: DIGITAL-2026-SKILLS-10-NATIONAL-COALITIONS This call focuses on building and expanding national ecosystems for digital skills development. Objectives Establish new National Coalitions in countries without existing frameworks Strengthen and expand existing coalitions and platforms Support national-level digital skills initiatives Who Should Apply Government bodies National organizations Digital skills platforms and coalitions Expected Impact Broader access to digital skills training Stronger collaboration across sectors Sustainable national digital ecosystems Important Events: Info Day Matchmaking Session To support applicants, an online Info Day and Matchmaking Event will be hosted: Date: 29 April 2026 Format: Online Purpose: Provide detailed insights into the calls Offer guidance on the application process Enable networking and partner discovery Participants will have the opportunity to connect with potential collaborators and explore partnership opportunities for project proposals. Why This Opportunity Matters The Digital Europe Programme is central to achieving the European Union’s 2030 Digital Decade goals , which aim to: Strengthen digital competencies across the workforce Drive innovation in critical sectors like healthcare and education Enhance Europe’s global competitiveness in digital technologies Projects funded under this call will directly contribute to: Accelerating AI adoption Advancing digital education solutions Building sustainable digital skills ecosystems How to Apply Click here for more information about this and to apply. Application Preparation Tips Identify the most relevant call for your organization Build strong partnerships across sectors Align your proposal with EU digital strategy goals Participate in the Info Day for deeper insights Engage in matchmaking sessions to find collaborators Final Thoughts This €12.5 million funding opportunity represents a major step forward in strengthening Europe’s digital future. Whether you are part of academia, industry, or government, this initiative offers a valuable platform to contribute to cutting-edge innovation and digital transformation across the continent. With a clear focus on AI, education technology, and digital skills ecosystems, these calls are set to shape the next generation of digital expertise in Europe. For more grant opportunities, Click here The post EU Digital Europe Programme 2026: €12.5 Million Funding for Advanced Digital Skills Projects Across Europe appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .
